corpus: Decided to migrate the backend from Node.js to Rust due to clear performance bottlenecks.
Storage layer migrated from RocksDB to redb — simpler compilation and pure Rust.
Selected Tantivy for full-text search — BM25 algorithm beats simple keyword matching.
The user prefers dark theme IDEs and dislikes bright backgrounds that strain the eyes.
The user is accustomed to using Vim editor for 10 years.
The user enjoys functional programming style and dislikes excessive mutable state.
The user prefers Rust for its safest type system.
The user prefers PostgreSQL over MySQL for its richer feature set.
HIPPMEM is a Rust-native associative memory engine providing long-term memory for AI agents.
hippmem uses redb for embedded storage, avoiding external database dependencies.
The engine supports 14 association edge types including entity overlap, topic overlap, and causal links.
Previously said the engine did not support tokenization, but it actually does via jieba.
Previously used REST API, later found gRPC to be better — corrected the architecture choice.
The user is John Chen, a senior software engineer based in San Francisco.
The user believes distributed system complexity is often underestimated — avoid distribution when a monolith suffices.
Discovered HNSW index slowdown at million-scale data — root cause was excessive distance calculations.
By adding IVF coarse quantization pre-filtering, HNSW search speed improved 5x.
Production deployment experienced OOM — memory spiked to 8GB causing Pod restart.
The weather is nice today, perfect for going out for a walk.
Had Kung Pao chicken for lunch, it was quite good.
hippmem_desc: HIPPMEM is a Rust-native associative memory engine providing long-term memory for AI agents.
